intent: TalentOrbit is a centralized human capital management platform designed to automate the full employee lifecycle, from recruitment and onboarding to performance appraisals and offboarding. Its unique value proposition lies in an AI-driven talent analytics dashboard that predicts turnover risk and matches internal skills against emerging project requirements, creating a seamless workflow that reduces administrative overhead by 40%.

entities.name: CandidatePipeline
entities.description: An immutable record representing the state machine of an applicant, tracking stages from sourcing to contract signature and background verification.

entities.name: SkillsTaxonomyGraph
entities.description: A graph-based data structure mapping individual employee proficiencies against organizational requirements and industry standard skill benchmarks.

entities.name: TurnoverRiskProfile
entities.description: An analytical projection object containing propensity scores and feature importance vectors generated by the AI inference engine for retention planning.

entities.name: PerformanceAppraisalRecord
entities.description: An encrypted, version-controlled audit trail of goal alignments, peer feedback, and manager evaluations integrated with compensation adjustment logs.

entities.name: PayrollIntegrationManifest
entities.description: A transactional bridge entity managing the reconciliation of HCM payroll data with third-party external accounting providers via RESTful webhooks.

entities.name: LifecycleEventTrigger
entities.description: An event-driven orchestration entity that automates task orchestration across provisioning, access control, and offboarding workflows.

architecture.description: The TalentOrbit architecture utilizes an event-driven microservices pattern on AWS/Azure, secured via an OIDC-compliant Identity Provider (IdP) for centralized SSO. Upon authentication, users interact with the responsive web front-end, which communicates with domain-specific microservices via an API Gateway protected by JWT validation and mTLS. The AI-driven predictive engine operates on a data pipeline that periodically syncs with the SkillsTaxonomyGraph to refresh TurnoverRiskProfile entities using Apache Flink for real-time stream processing. Payroll and accounting integrations are managed by the PayrollIntegrationManifest, which utilizes a circuit-breaker pattern to ensure robust communication with external legacy systems. All PII is segregated into a compliant data vault to ensure GDPR/SOC2 adherence, with database-level encryption at rest and field-level encryption for sensitive appraisal data. The LifecycleEventTrigger coordinates cross-functional service interactions via a message broker (e.g., Kafka or Service Bus), ensuring high availability and ACID compliance across the employee lifecycle.

endpoints.summary: Retrieve the current state machine of an applicant
Update the pipeline stage for a candidate
validation_rules: current_stage must be a non-empty string

rules.description: Ensure accurate tracking of candidate pipeline updates by verifying status transitions
rules.logic: IF CandidatePipeline.current_stage is NOT EMPTY THEN update LifecycleEventTrigger.execution_status TO 'processed'

rules.description: Trigger turnover risk refresh upon performance appraisal updates
rules.logic: IF PerformanceAppraisalRecord.record_id EXISTS THEN sync TurnoverRiskProfile via SkillsTaxonomyGraph
